description_long: This house complex features a dense arrangement of interconnected residential units with flat white facades and several prominent terracotta-tiled gable and hip roofs. The structure is set along a paved urban street, with palm trees and sidewalks lining the perimeter, and multiple vehicles parked nearby. The building massing is blocky and geometric, with a mix of single and two-story sections, and the roofs provide visual contrast with their warm, earthy tones. Windows and doors are distributed asymmetrically, with some units featuring recessed balconies or small overhangs. The overall design suggests a Mediterranean or contemporary urban style, with a focus on maximizing space and privacy within a compact footprint. The exterior materials appear to be primarily stucco or concrete, with tiled roofing and glass for windows.
description: A dense, blocky residential house complex with white facades, terracotta-tiled roofs, and asymmetrical window placement, set along a palm-lined urban street.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main street-facing facade, terracotta roofs, and palm trees lining the sidewalk.
description_view_1: Rear view highlights the back of the complex, with more visible flat roof sections and less street detail.
description_view_2: Opposite street view reveals additional terracotta roof sections and the mirrored street layout.
description_view_3: Backside view emphasizes the blocky massing and flat roof areas, with minimal street features.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryways are recessed with simple doors, some featuring small overhangs or porches, and minimal decorative elements.
